skill.name: explore-run
skill.description: Rigor Improve / Rigor Explore run leaf skill for bounded exploratory evidence in deep learning research repositories. Use when the researcher explicitly authorizes exploratory runs such as small-subset validation, short-cycle guess-and-check, batch sweeps, idle-GPU search, or quick transfer-learning trials, with fair-comparison caveats and no-overclaim summaries in `explore_outputs/`. Do not use for end-to-end exploration orchestration on top of `current_research`, trusted baseline execution, conservative training verification, default routing, verified SOTA claims, or implicit experimentation.
skill.summary: Bounded exploratory run planning for deep learning research, with fair-comparison caveats and budget-aware variant ranking.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: All 45 static findings are false positives from pattern matching on markdown formatting. The 'backticks' are inline code formatting in documentation, 'path traversal sequences' are markdown file path references, and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches found no actual crypto operations in the 284-line Python script. The skill is a legitimate research methodology tool for planning bounded exploratory experiments in deep learning, with a Python script that performs variant scoring and output generation using only JSON read/write operations.

low_findings.title: Markdown inline code formatting flagged as backtick execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 34 instances of backtick characters in SKILL.md and execution-policy.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. On manual review, all instances are standard markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `current_research`, `explore_outputs/`) used in documentation, not shell command execution.

low_findings.title: Relative path references flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged relative path references like `../../references/` in SKILL.md as path traversal sequences. These are legitimate documentation references to sibling directories in the skill repository, not filesystem access patterns with user-controlled input.

content.user_title: Plan bounded exploratory deep learning experiments
content.value_statement: Deep learning researchers need a structured way to run exploratory experiments without overclaiming results. This skill provides budget-aware variant ranking and fair-comparison summaries for bounded exploratory runs.
actual_capabilities: Generates budget-aware variant matrices from a JSON exploration spec using `scripts/plan_variants.py`
Ranks candidate runs by cost, success rate, and expected gain with configurable weights
Prunes variants to respect `max_variants` and `max_short_cycle_runs` budget constraints
Writes structured output bundles (CHANGESET.md, TOP_RUNS.md, status.json) to `explore_outputs/`
Isolates exploratory runs from the trusted baseline per execution policy
limitations: Only handles experiment planning and summary generation, not actual training execution
Requires explicit researcher authorization before applying
Cannot be used for trusted baseline execution or verified SOTA claims
Script-based output assumes a compatible Python environment and shared module structure

use_cases.title: Quick small-subset validation probes
use_cases.description: Run a small-subset training probe to validate a hypothesis before committing to a full training run.

use_cases.title: Batch sweep candidate ranking
use_cases.description: Generate and rank a batch of candidate hyperparameter combinations for an exploratory sweep.

use_cases.title: Idle-GPU search planning
use_cases.description: Plan bounded exploratory runs that fit within available GPU idle time windows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic exploratory run planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a bounded exploratory run for my deep learning experiment. Use explore-run to generate a variant matrix from my spec file at specs/explore.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: When a researcher wants to plan a small exploratory sweep

prompt_templates.title: Budget-constrained sweep
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a GPU budget of 2 hours. Use explore-run to plan a short-cycle sweep with max_variants=4 and max_short_cycle_runs=2.
prompt_templates.scenario: When working under tight compute constraints

prompt_templates.title: Transfer learning trial planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a quick transfer-learning trial using explore-run. Prioritize low-cost candidates with high expected gain. Write outputs to explore_outputs/.

prompt_templates.title: Fair-comparison summary generation
prompt_templates.prompt: After running exploratory variants, use explore-run to generate a COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md that clearly labels which comparisons are not directly fair.
prompt_templates.scenario: When documenting exploratory results for a paper or report

output_examples.input: Plan an exploratory run with 3 learning rates, 2 batch sizes, and max_variants=4
output: Generated variant matrix with 4 selected candidates from 6 raw variants
Output written to explore_outputs/CHANGESET.md, explore_outputs/TOP_RUNS.md, and explore_outputs/status.json
Each variant annotated with cost_score, predicted_success_score, and total_score

output_examples.input: Compare two model architectures on a small subset
output: COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md highlights that the comparison is bounded evidence only
SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md records the exploratory branch and variant count
Results explicitly labeled as non-trusted before human review

best_practices: Always set explicit `max_variants` and `max_short_cycle_runs` budget limits in the exploration spec
Use default selection weights unless the researcher provides explicit `selection_weights`
Label all exploratory results as bounded evidence and avoid promoting them to trusted baseline automatically
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for trusted baseline execution or conservative training verification
Do not run exploratory experiments without explicit researcher authorization
Do not claim SOTA or trusted results from exploratory outputs

faq.question: What is the difference between explore-run and ai-research-explore?
faq.answer: explore-run is a leaf skill focused on exploratory run planning and summary. ai-research-explore handles end-to-end exploration orchestration that spans both current_research coordination and exploratory code changes.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for trusted baseline training?
faq.answer: No. This skill is strictly for bounded exploratory runs. Use run-train or minimal-run-and-audit for trusted baseline execution.

faq.question: How does variant scoring work?
faq.answer: Variants are scored using three factors: cost, success rate, and expected gain. Each factor is derived from step count, subset size, and axis aggressiveness. Default weights are 0.25, 0.35, and 0.40 respectively.

faq.question: What output files does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces CHANGESET.md, SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md, COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md, TOP_RUNS.md, and status.json inside the explore_outputs/ directory.

faq.question: Does the skill execute training jobs?
faq.answer: No. The skill only plans and summarizes exploratory runs. It may hand off actual command execution to minimal-run-and-audit or run-train skills.

faq.question: Is the skill compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to work with all three tools, with an OpenAI agent configuration provided in agents/openai.yaml.
